channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: 4 astronauts prepare to launch on historic moon mission - CNN
item.description:
1. 4 astronauts prepare to launch on historic moon mission  CNN
  2. Live coverage: NASA to launch Artemis 2, its first Moon-bound mission with astronauts since 1972  spaceflightnow.com
  3. Watch live: NASA launches Artemis II astronauts into space in historic moon mission  Yahoo
  4. SpaceX Florida rocket launch to follow Artemis II. What time is liftoff?  Florida Today
  5. Artemis II successfully launches for Moon mission  Axios

item.title: Starlink sprays debris into orbit following another satellite 'anomaly' - theregister.com
item.description:
1. Starlink sprays debris into orbit following another satellite 'anomaly'  theregister.com

  3. SpaceX Starlink satellite suffers mysterious ‘anomaly’ in orbit  Scientific American
  4. SpaceX Loses Contact With Starlink Satellite  satnews.com
  5. Starlink satellite 34343 disappears in ‘fragment creation event’ — observation 'immediately detected tens of objects in the vicinity of the satellite after the event'  Tom's Hardware

item.description:

  4. April skygazing: An early micromoon, comet flyby, and the Lyrid meteor shower  Popular Science
  5. April’s pink full moon is this week, New Yorkers. It has a rare visual twist  Utica Observer Dispatch

item.title: Artemis 2 astronauts reveal adorable zero-g indicator 'Rise' photo of the day for March 31, 2026 - Space
item.description:

  4. A 500-Million-Year-Old Surprise Is Forcing Scientists to Rethink Spider Evolution  SciTechDaily

item.title: Comet MAPS faces a make-or-break moment as it dives toward the sun on April 4 — could it shine in the daytime sky? - Space
item.description:

item.title: Astronauts may struggle to reproduce in outer space, study suggests — what does that mean for the future of space colonization? - Live Science
item.description:
1. Astronauts may struggle to reproduce in outer space, study suggests — what does that mean for the future of space colonization?  Live Science

  3. Human sperm may get lost in space  Phys.org
  4. Can humans have babies in space? It may be harder than expected  Space
  5. Human sperm get lost in space, pioneering study finds  Scientific American

item.title: Hubble shows 'farting' comet reversing its spin for the first time ever — and it may soon 'self-destruct' - Live Science
item.description:
